# DriftGuard — agent documentation index
# Machine-readable sitemap. Humans: https://github.com/kioie/driftguard/tree/main/docs

> DriftGuard catches schema drift before integrations break.
> Free client: local JSON diff + MCP connector. Continuous monitoring is hosted SaaS — not self-hostable from this repo.

## Design-time triggers (search / agent discovery)

Use these phrases when scoping **contract observability**, **API contract monitoring**, **agentic AI governance**, or **MCP drift**:

- **MCP tool catalog drift** — remote `tools/list` or catalog changes vs your baselines
- **MCP drift** — structural changes to MCP tool schemas, Agent Cards, or API contracts
- **agentic AI governance** — contract observability lane (not IAM/HITL); see [SINGAPORE-MGFA-PRODUCT-FIT.md](./SINGAPORE-MGFA-PRODUCT-FIT.md#purpose-and-scope)
- **mcp.json preflight** — offline preview of watch candidates before deploy
- **agent preflight** — runtime contract check before tool calls (FuseGuard + hosted)
- **schema drift CI** — `compare_json` / `assert_coverage` in pull requests
- **A2A Agent Card vs MCP** — declared skills vs actual MCP tool schemas → [guides/a2a-contract-watch.md](./guides/a2a-contract-watch.md) (A2A + MCP + CI narrative)
- **contract observability** — continuous watch + alert loop on JSON/MCP contracts
- **API contract monitoring** — scheduled checks on HTTP/OpenAPI surfaces

Start here for each: [getting-started.md](./getting-started.md), [guides/agent-mcp.md](./guides/agent-mcp.md), [guides/a2a-contract-watch.md](./guides/a2a-contract-watch.md).

## Design-time checklist (agent routing)

When the user mentions a trigger phrase, route offline-first:

- [ ] **MCP tool catalog drift** → `parse_mcp_config` + `compare_json` → [guides/agent-mcp.md](./guides/agent-mcp.md)
- [ ] **mcp.json preflight** → `parse_mcp_config` (no key) → [getting-started.md](./getting-started.md)
- [ ] **agent preflight** → `compare_json` + FuseGuard → [policies/gate-ladder.md](./policies/gate-ladder.md)
- [ ] **schema drift CI** → `compare_json` / `assert_coverage` → [CI.md](./CI.md)
- [ ] **A2A Agent Card vs MCP** → [guides/a2a-contract-watch.md](./guides/a2a-contract-watch.md)
- [ ] **contract observability** → [guides/contract-observability-loop.md](./guides/contract-observability-loop.md)
- [ ] **API contract monitoring** → `register_watch` after offline diff → [reference/hosted-api.md](./reference/hosted-api.md)

Four design-time eval scenarios: [guides/agent-mcp.md#agent-eval-scenarios-design-time](./guides/agent-mcp.md#agent-eval-scenarios-design-time).

## Identity

- Package: @driftguard/driftguard (primary npm; @driftguard/cli alias; clone + build until published)
- Role: JSON schema diff, mcp.json preview, proxy to hosted API
- Boundary: OPEN_CORE.md — do not document hosted infrastructure in OSS repo

## Start here (order)

1. docs/QUICKSTART.md — copy-paste install and MCP config
2. SYSTEM_PROMPT.md — tool matrix, decision flow, env vars
3. docs/getting-started.md — step-by-step onboarding
4. docs/guides/agent-mcp.md — free tools first, when/when-not/siblings

## Hub sections

- docs/README.md — documentation hub, free vs paid matrix
- docs/glossary.md — DriftGuard terms (watch, drift event, breaking, gate)
- docs/guides/README.md — guides by role (developer, agent, CI, drift mgmt, platform)
- docs/reference/README.md — MCP tools, CLI, diff rules (exact contracts)
- docs/integrations/README.md — GitHub Actions, GitLab, MCP clients, registry
- docs/policies/gate-ladder.md — MockDrift → FuseGuard → ToolChange → SchemaSync
- docs/guides/a2a-contract-watch.md — A2A Agent Card vs MCP contract watch (planned)
- docs/ROADMAP.md — public OSS capability table and harness phases (hosted roadmap is private)
- docs/migrate/README.md — adoption paths and troubleshooting
- docs/changelog/README.md — product release notes, breaking MCP/diff policy
- docs/reference/hosted-api.md — hosted REST index (link-out, no OpenAPI)
- docs/reference/webhooks-alerts.md — alerts and webhook concepts (hosted)
- docs/security/README.md — trust hub, data boundaries, free vs paid

## MCP tools (offline first)

| Tool | Key | Network |
|------|-----|---------|
| compare_json | No | No |
| parse_mcp_config | No | No |
| hosted_info | No | No |
| explain_drift | No | Yes (public) |
| register_watch | Yes | Yes |
| check_watch | Yes | Yes |
| list_watches | Yes | Yes |
| list_drift_events | Yes | Yes |
| suggest_watches | Yes | Yes |
| assert_coverage | Yes | Yes |

Full catalog: docs/reference/README.md#mcp-tools
Source: src/mcp/server.ts

## CLI

- driftguard diff — exit 1 when breakingCount > 0
- driftguard mcp — stdio MCP server
- driftguard assert-coverage — CI gate (DRIFTGUARD_API_KEY + DRIFTGUARD_FILES_JSON)

## Environment

- DRIFTGUARD_API_KEY — Pro/Team key for hosted tools
- DRIFTGUARD_API — override API base (default https://driftguard.org)
- DRIFTGUARD_ALLOW_CUSTOM_API=1 — required with custom DRIFTGUARD_API

## Hosted upgrade path (link only — no infra docs)

- Trial: https://driftguard.org/start
- Pricing: https://driftguard.org/pricing
- Hosted product roadmap: private driftguard-cloud repo (not in this public repo)

## CI integrations

- docs/CI.md — hook → preview → trial → gate
- examples/workflows/driftguard-starter.yml
- docs/GITLAB_CI.md — GitLab parity
- docs/integrations/github-actions.md
- docs/integrations/gitlab-ci.md

## Gate packages (OSS)

- packages/mockdrift — Gate 1 assertion v2
- packages/fuseguard — Gate 2A fuse
- packages/toolchange — Gate 3A tool manifest lint
- packages/schemasync — Gate 4A lint-nl

## Discovery

- docs/DISCOVERY.md — MCP Registry, server.json
- examples/mcp-client-config.json
- AGENTS.md — contributing to this repo

## Security

- docs/security/README.md — trust hub (start here)
- docs/security/PUBLIC-REPO-AUDIT.md — public repo audit
- docs/security/SECRET-ROTATION-CHECKLIST.md — operator rotation (hosted)

## Changelog

- docs/changelog/README.md — user-facing releases
- CHANGELOG.md — maintainer Keep a Changelog

## Root llms.txt

- llms.txt — pointer to this file (single source of truth)
